evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java (1042 lines, main REST API) has zero test coverage — no test file exists for it
SemanticFieldExplorer.java (690 lines, core business logic for semantic exploration) has zero test coverage
PatternSubstitution.java, HttpApiUtils.java, BlackLabSnippetParser.java, RadialPlot.java all have zero test coverage
BlackLabDummyTest, DependencySketchTest, BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest are hardcoded to D:\corpora_philsci\bi and always skip in any CI/developer environment without that path
CQLParserTest covers 25+ parsing scenarios thoroughly including edge cases and real grammar patterns
LogDiceCalculatorTest covers edge cases, symmetry, monotonicity, overflow protection, and real corpus scenarios
PredicatesTest and TokenAndWindowTest cover predicate combinations and token window boundary behavior
14 test files exist for 30 production files, but the 14 test files are concentrated on utilities/parsing rather than core business logic

strengths: CQLParserTest provides thorough coverage of parsing: 25+ test cases covering constraints, wildcards, distance operators, OR/AND/NOT combinators, regex-in-value patterns, and real grammar patterns
LogDiceCalculatorTest covers edge cases (zero inputs, negative values, overflow), symmetry property, monotonicity, and real corpus scenarios with explicit expected ranges
PredicatesTest and TokenAndWindowTest verify logical predicate combinations (And, Or, Not) and window boundary clamping with concrete assertions
Environment-gated integration tests (BlackLabDummyTest, DependencySketchTest) use JUnit Assumptions correctly so they skip gracefully rather than fail in CI
test_strategy.issue_character: Testing effort is concentrated on low-risk utility and parsing code while the two largest, most complex production classes (WordSketchApiServer at 1042 lines and SemanticFieldExplorer at 690 lines) have zero coverage; the only tests touching the BlackLab executor are permanently skipped in any environment without a specific local Windows path.
test_strategy.score_rationale: The codebase sits well below the 60 threshold for 'significant drag'. While the utility/parsing layer has genuinely good tests (CQLParser, LogDice, Predicates), the critical paths — the REST API server, semantic field exploration algorithm, snippet parsing, and pattern substitution — have no tests at all. The always-skipping integration tests provide false confidence: they look like coverage but never execute. This is not uneven quality (80-range); it is a structural gap where the highest-complexity, highest-risk code is entirely unverified.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer (1042 lines, main REST API) has zero test coverage
evidence: The file is 1042 lines containing all REST endpoint handlers, request routing, error handling, and response formatting — all untested
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests using a mock QueryExecutor to test request routing, parameter validation, error responses, and JSON serialisation for each endpoint; use MockitoAnnotations or manual stubs since the class accepts QueryExecutor via constructor

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer (690 lines, core semantic logic) has zero test coverage
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer contains the multi-seed exploration algorithm (intersection logic, scoring, thresholding) but no tests verify its correctness
issues.suggestion: Create SemanticFieldExplorerTest using a stub QueryExecutor that returns controlled WordSketchResult lists; test intersection logic, minimum shared threshold filtering, and edge cases (empty seed, no overlap, single seed)

issues.summary: Integration tests using BlackLab are hardcoded to a local Windows path and always skip outside that environment
evidence: All three files contain: String INDEX_PATH = "D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi"
Tests use Assumptions.assumeTrue(indexDir.exists()) or @EnabledIf("indexExists"), so they never execute in any developer or CI environment without that exact path
This creates an illusion of integration coverage where none actually executes
issues.suggestion: Replace with tests that build a small in-memory BlackLab index from embedded test CoNLL-U data (a few sentences) using BlackLab's testing utilities, so integration tests always run without external dependencies

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser has no tests despite parsing complex XML into structured results
evidence: Snippet parsing is a fragile text-processing step where correctness bugs would silently corrupt query results
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests with representative BlackLab XML snippet strings as test fixtures; verify that head positions, collocate lemmas, and sentence text are extracted correctly

root_causes: Test effort appears to have been applied bottom-up (utilities → parsers) but never extended to the integration and API layers
The BlackLab executor dependency makes integration testing non-trivial, which likely discouraged testing of any class that touches it, including SemanticFieldExplorer and WordSketchApiServer
